page: Hetzner Cloud cx53 is an x86_64 virtual server powered by AMD EPYC-Rome processors. It features 16 shared vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, backed by 16 physical cores with 1 thread per core. The system is configured with 31.0 GB of RAM, providing approximately 1.95 GB of memory per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 320 GB SSD, and the virtualization is managed via the QEMU hypervisor.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core scoring 2667.99, placing it in the top 25% of tested instances. Multi-core performance is average, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 42844.81 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 10997.0. Memory bandwidth is consistent, recording 128.82 GB/sec for large block read operations and 70.57 GB/sec for write operations. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performs strongly at 168.63 GB/sec, while LLM text generation using a small 135M model also shows strong performance at 276.25 tokens/sec.

Given its shared CPU allocation, the cx53 offers a cost-efficient resource profile for workloads that require high parallel processing capacity without the premium of dedicated cores. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it suitable for compute-heavy tasks rather than memory-intensive databases. Appropriate workloads include multi-threaded file decompression, software compilation, static web serving, and moderate LLM inference tasks.
description: Hetzner Cloud cx53 is an x86_64 virtual server configured with 16 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 31.0 GB of RAM, and a 320 GB local SSD. Operating on the QEMU hypervisor, this instance delivers strong single-core CPU performance and multi-threaded decompression capabilities, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, it represents a cost-efficient option for compute-focused workloads that do not require dedicated CPU resources. The hardware supports AVX, AES, and SHA_NI instructions, enhancing cryptographic and vector processing. It achieves 30.09 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 22.86 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. This profile makes it a balanced choice for general compute tasks, web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cx53 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs, 31.0 GB of RAM, and 320 GB of local SSD storage, delivering strong single-core and multi-threaded decompression performance.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud cx53 is an x86_64 virtual server with 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 31.0 GB of RAM, and a 320 GB local SSD on the QEMU hypervisor.
tagline: A cost-efficient, compute-focused virtual server featuring sixteen shared AMD EPYC cores, balanced memory, and local SSD storage for parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 16 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs and 31.0 GB of RAM
Strong single-core CPU and multi-threaded decompression performance
Hardware-accelerated cryptography with AES and SHA_NI support
Optimized for software compilation, web serving, and parallel data processing
